records.ety: [OE. speken, AS. specan, sprecan; akin to OF.ries. spreka, D. spreken, OS. spreken, G. sprechen, OHG. sprehhan, and perhaps to Skr. sphūrj to crackle, to thunder. Cf. Spark of fire, Speech.]
records.morph: [imp. Spoke (?) (Spake (<?/) Archaic); p. p. Spoken (?) (Spoke, Obs. or Colloq.); p. pr. & vb. n. Speaking.]

defs: To utter words or articulate sounds, as human beings; to express thoughts by words; as, the organs may be so obstructed that a man may not be able to speak.
quotes: Till at the last spake in this manner.
Speak, Lord; for thy servant heareth.

defs: To express opinions; to say; to talk; to converse.
quotes: That fluid substance in a few minutes begins to set, as the tradesmen speak.
An honest man, is able to speak for himself, when a knave is not.
During the century and a half which followed the Conquest, there is, to speak strictly, no English history.

defs: To utter a speech, discourse, or harangue; to adress a public assembly formally.
quotes: Many of the nobility made themselves popular by speaking in Parliament against those things which were most grateful to his majesty.

defs: To discourse; to make mention; to tell.
quotes: Lycan speaks of a part of Caesar's army that came to him from the Leman Lake.

defs: To convey sentiments, ideas, or intelligence as if by utterance; as, features that speak of self-will.
To utter with the mouth; to pronounce; to utter articulately, as human beings.
quotes: They sat down with him upn ground seven days and seven nights, and none spake a word unto him.

defs: To utter in a word or words; to say; to tell; to declare orally; as, to speak the truth; to speak sense.
To declare; to proclaim; to publish; to make known; to exhibit; to express in any way.
quotes: It is my father;s muste To speak your deeds.
Speaking a still good morrow with her eyes.
And for the heaven's wide circuit, let it speak The maker's high magnificence.
Report speaks you a bonny monk.

defs: To talk or converse in; to utter or pronounce, as in conversation; as, to speak Latin.
quotes: And French she spake full fair and fetisely.

defs: To address; to accost; to speak to.
quotes: [He will] thee in hope; he will speak thee fair.
each village senior paused to scan And speak the lovely caravan.
